body:
## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking` **Service directory:** `sdk/servicenetworking/Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking/` **Triggered by:** PR #58483 (commit 60f88fae6) by @ArcturusZhang ## What Changed Introduced new provisioning library `Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking` (1.0.0-beta.1) with support for four Microsoft.ServiceNetworking resources: `TrafficController`, `TrafficControllerFrontend`, `TrafficControllerAssociation`, and `ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy`. ## Gaps Found - **Empty "Authenticate the client" section** — The README has the section heading but no content; provisioning libraries don't require an `AzureCredential` in the same way but should note the `azd` / `AzureDeveloperCliCredential` usage pattern - **Empty "Next steps" section** — No links to related samples, Azure documentation for Application Gateway for Containers, or the `Azure.Provisioning` README - **`ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy` is undocumented** — The CHANGELOG lists this resource but the README Examples section has no example or mention of it; a snippet showing how to create and attach a security policy to a traffic controller is missing
📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files **File:** `sdk/servicenetworking/Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking/README.md` (edit) 1. **Fill in "Authenticate the client" section** — Replace the empty body under `### Authenticate the Client` with content similar to the pattern used in other provisioning packages (e.g., `sdk/storage/Azure.Provisioning.Storage/README.md`): ```markdown ### Authenticate the Client This library provisions Azure infrastructure using [Azure Developer CLI (`azd`)](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/developer/azure-developer-cli/overview). Authentication is handled automatically by `azd` during deployment. For local development or CI/CD, you can use `AzureDeveloperCliCredential` or any other credential from the [`Azure.Identity`](https://www.nuget.org/packages/Azure.Identity) package. ``` 2. **Fill in "Next steps" section** — Replace the empty body under `## Next steps` with useful links: ```markdown ## Next steps - [Azure Application Gateway for Containers documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/application-gateway/for-containers/overview) - [Azure.Provisioning overview](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/provisioning/Azure.Provisioning/README.md) - [Azure Developer CLI (`azd`) documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/developer/azure-developer-cli/overview) ``` 3. **Add an `ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy` example** — After the existing `### Create a Traffic Controller` example, add: ```markdown ### Create a Security Policy This example demonstrates how to create an `ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy` and associate it with a Traffic Controller. ```C# Snippet:ServiceNetworkingSecurityPolicy ``` ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets **File to create:** `sdk/servicenetworking/Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking/tests/BasicServiceNetworkingTests.cs` (edit — add a new test method) Add the following method to the existing `BasicServiceNetworkingTests` class, below `CreateTrafficController()`: ```csharp internal static Trycep CreateSecurityPolicyTest() { return new Trycep().Define( ctx => { #region Snippet:ServiceNetworkingSecurityPolicy Infrastructure infra = new(); TrafficController controller = new(nameof(controller), TrafficController.ResourceVersions.V2025_01_01) { Tags = { ["environment"] = "test" }, }; infra.Add(controller); ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy policy = new(nameof(policy), ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy.ResourceVersions.V2025_01_01) { Parent = controller, }; infra.Add(policy); #endregion return infra; }); } [Test] public async Task CreateSecurityPolicy() { await using Trycep test = CreateSecurityPolicyTest(); // Expected Bicep output verified by test runner test.Compare( """ @description('The location for the resource(s) to be deployed.') param location string = resourceGroup().location resource controller 'Microsoft.ServiceNetworking/trafficControllers@2025-01-01' = { name: take('controller-${uniqueString(resourceGroup().id)}', 24) location: location } resource policy 'Microsoft.ServiceNetworking/trafficControllers/securityPolicies@2025-01-01' = { name: take('policy-${uniqueString(resourceGroup().id)}', 24) location: location parent: controller } """); } ``` > **Note:** Verify the exact Bicep resource type path for `ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy` by inspecting `src/Generated/ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy.cs` and its `ResourceVersions` inner class, then adjust the `test.Compare(...)` expected string accordingly. **Snippet name:** `ServiceNetworkingSecurityPolicy` **README location:** After the closing ` ``` ` of the `ServiceNetworkingBasic` snippet block, under `## Examples` ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README at `sdk/servicenetworking/Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking/README.md` currently has these sections: | Section | Status | |---|---| | Getting started → Install the package | ✅ Present | | Getting started → Prerequisites | ✅ Present | | Getting started → Authenticate the client | ❌ Empty — needs content | | Key concepts | ✅ Present | | Examples | ⚠️ Partial — missing security policy example | | Troubleshooting | ✅ Present | | Next steps | ❌ Empty — needs links | | Contributing | ✅ Present | ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order. Each must succeed before proceeding to the next: 1. `dotnet build sdk/servicenetworking/Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/servicenetworking/Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 servicenetworking` — injects `#region Snippet:` blocks from test files into README placeholders; check `git diff` afterwards to confirm README was updated 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 servicenetworking` — regenerates public API listing files (only needed if public types changed) 5. `dotnet format sdk/servicenetworking/Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking/`
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **@copilot** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above
title: [ServiceNetworking] Docs: Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking README gaps
